events.title: England vs. Ghana - World Cup - All You can Drink!
events.description:
There is NO cover charge to watch the match. $5 Beers All Match Long Just come in, grab a seat, and enjoy the game on our big screens with fellow soccer fans.


Looking to make it an even bigger party?

 

Optional Upgrade: $45 All-You-Can-Drink Package

For one flat price, enjoy unlimited drinks throughout the match and experience the World Cup the EastVille way.

Why Watch at EastVille?

  • Giant screens and premium game viewing
  • Full bar and drink specials
  • Electric World Cup atmosphere
  • Indoor comfort with plenty of seating
  • Soccer fans from around the city

Kickoff is at 3:00 PM, so arrive early, grab a drink, and get ready for an unforgettable World Cup afternoon.


USA vs Australia • Friday, June 19 • 3PM

events.title: Mexico v Czechia Outdoor World Cup Watch Party
events.description:
Come watch these two countries face each other on our 15-foot wide screen!
Come watch Mexico take on Czechia in group match competition on our 15-foot wide projection screen and listen to the game in full concert sound under the stars in Sunset Park, Brooklyn.
This event requires a $5 RSVP, which includes a $6 discount on your first order at our venue bar.
There will be drink specials but don’t forget to take advantage of the 2-for-1 drink ticket when you check out! Space is limited and RSVP doesn’t guarantee entry. Buying advanced drink tickets guarantees entry!
NO STROLLERS or children under the age of 3.
Limited seating (about 100 seats), tons of standing room. SO THAT WE MAY INCLUDE AS MANY PEOPLE AS POSSIBLE AND FOR YOUR SAFETY, PLEASE DO NOT BRING YOUR OWN CHAIRS. Trust us, the second the game starts everybody is up and cheering anyway!
While we do our best to gauge the number of RSVPs, entry is still on a first-come, first-served basis, so please arrive early! Once we hit capacity, we won’t be able to let anyone else in.
No outside beverages. There will be drink specials all day long from our courtyard bar. Feel free to bring in food from the adjoining food hall!
Event Info
Date: June 24, 2026
Doors: 8:00pm
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device.
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.

events.title: Community Center Showcase
events.description:
SHOWCASE! Creativity In Community
The Isaac Brown Healing Arts & Community Center invites Brooklyn to experience a living showcase of creativity, wellness, and human connection.
More than an event, SHOWCASE! is an open invitation into the heart of our community, a chance to explore the workshops, groups, and healing-centered experiences happening inside our center every day, while helping shape what comes next.
Throughout the day, guests will experience a curated mix of:
• Creative arts workshops
• Live music & jam sessions
• Wellness and mindfulness experiences
• Writing, movement, and expression-based groups
• Interactive community conversations
• Emerging ideas and future program concepts
Whether you’re interested in poetry, music exploration, photography, improv, mindful support, sketching, gardening, podcasting, candle making, veterans groups, or simply finding a space where you feel seen, The Center SHOWCASE creates room for discovery without pressure.
This event is designed for people to wander, experience, connect, and participate at their own pace.
We believe healing can happen through creativity.
We believe community should be accessible.
And we believe people deserve spaces where curiosity is welcomed.
Come discover something you didn’t know you needed.
Come contribute your voice.
Come help shape the future of community wellness through art.
June 25th
11 AM – 4 PM
Isaac Brown Healing Arts & Community Center
882 3rd Ave, Brooklyn, NY

events.title: Floating Cloister: AI, Pace & Community — A Freelancer’s Gathering
events.description:
A six-hour, in-person gathering on June 25 for fifty freelancers who are building their careers deliberately — using AI thoughtfully, protecting their pace, and looking for genuine peers (not networking contacts).
Whether you’re a designer, writer, consultant, builder, or in-between-things, this room is for solo workers who want to think more carefully about the work they’re making, the tools they’re using, and the practice they’re building.

events.title: The PS10 House Band Presents Tribute to the 90s
events.description:
Industry City and Get It Right Music in association with Murmrr Presents are proud to welcome the PS10 House Band for an evening of 90s Live Music – Sing, Dance & Relive the Hits!
Join us for an unforgettable night of live music celebrating your favorite songs from the 1990s! From Britney to Alanis, Pearl Jam to The B-52’s, TLC to Montell Jordan—and most things in between—the PS10 House Band will be playing hit after hit from one of the greatest decades in music.
Come dance, sing along, or just soak in the nostalgia with friends.
Born out of the parent community of a local elementary school (yes, PS10), the PS10 House Band’s dynamic lineup and versatility allows them to move effortlessly across genres, delivering an energetic and crowd-pleasing performance.
Event Info
Date: June 25, 2026
Doors: 5:45pm
Showtime: 6pm
Tickets: $20 per person in advance/$25 day of show plus fees
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.

events.title: Puppetworks: Behind the Curtain KIDS!
events.description:
Celebrate the magic of Puppetworks at this special family-friendly event as Brooklyn’s beloved marionette theatre prepares to open its new home at Industry City.
Children and families are invited to enjoy an afternoon of interactive puppet activities, creative workshops, hands-on crafts, special displays, and opportunities to meet Puppetworks artists and performers. Discover the artistry behind puppetry, explore the world of storytelling, and help celebrate nearly 50 years of imagination and live theatre that have inspired generations of Brooklyn families.
Perfect for children of all ages, this free community event offers a fun and memorable way to experience the wonder of Puppetworks and get a first look at its exciting future at Industry City.
Friday, June 26, 2026 | 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM
The Tom Kane Theatre at brooklynONE productions, Industry City

events.title: Puppetworks: Behind the Curtain — Community Reception & Future Home Preview
events.description:
Join Industry City, brooklynONE productions, and Puppetworks for a special evening celebrating one of Brooklyn’s most beloved cultural institutions as it prepares to begin its next chapter at Industry City.
This community reception will feature wine and light refreshments, special presentations, community storytelling, a live puppet performance, and an exclusive preview of Puppetworks’ future home. Guests will have the opportunity to tour the new theatre and educational space, hear reflections from artists and supporters, and help commemorate nearly 50 years of imagination, education, and live performance that have touched generations of Brooklyn families.
Whether you grew up attending Puppetworks performances, have supported its mission, or simply believe in the importance of preserving Brooklyn’s cultural institutions, this event offers a unique opportunity to celebrate its legacy and future.
Friday, June 26, 2026 | 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM
The Tom Kane Theatre at brooklynONE productions, Industry City

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: Brotaku: Tournament Comedy Show
events.description:
Stand-up comedians roast players while they compete live in a chaotic Mario Kart tournament.
16 competitors enter. One leaves with $250. Everyone else gets cooked.
Expect:
• stand-up to open the show
• player intros + live roasting
• wildcard players pulled from the audience
• unhinged energy the entire time
Part tournament, part comedy show, part public execution.
Like playing video games on your friend’s couch—if your friends were ruthless comedians.

Details:
• Friday, June 27
• Doors 5:00 PM | Show 6:00 PM
• Eastville Comedy Club (Brooklyn)
• 2 drink minimum

Tickets:
• Competitor Pass ($27): guaranteed tournament spot
• Audience ($19): watch + chance to be pulled as a wildcard
Competitor slots fill fast. Last show sold out.
All tickets include 2 takoyaki balls + 1 meat skewer from Karl’s Balls and Izakaya Juraku.

events.title: Balkan Comedy Night
events.description:
Balkan Comedy Night is a nationally-touring show featuring the funniest up-and-coming comedians from the US, Canada and the Balkans! The show makes jokes about our cultures, families, languages, history, and shared experiences. The show is in English with Balkan references. Come laugh at jokes that are straight from the motherland!   


Edmond Çami is a stand-up comedian known for his sharp, witty, and observational humor. Originally from Pogradec, Albania, he began his comedy career in 2015 after making his debut in South Carolina, where he still resides. Drawing from his experiences navigating both Albanian and American cultures, he offers a unique comedic perspective that resonates with diverse audiences. His humor often stems from the cultural contrasts he explores, turning everyday questions and observations into clever, relatable comedy.


Chris Tos (also known as Christos Petropopoudopoulos) is a New York City–based stand-up comedian, writer, and director known for his high-energy, character-driven comedy. Drawing inspiration from his Greek and Albanian heritage, as well as pop culture and nerd culture, he delivers fast-paced, satirical performances that resonate with a wide audience. Chris has built a strong online following through viral videos on TikTok and Instagram, amassing over 80 million views with popular sketches like “How to Find an Emo Kid in a Crowd” and “Going Super Saiyan 2 in Public.”


Ryan Radusinovic is a 12 year comedy veteran from Los Angeles and the founder of Balkan Comedy Night. He has been featured on CBS, Amazon Prime, Just For Laughs and Netflix is a Joke Festival. His style blends animated story-telling, pitch perfect impressions, clever observations and merciless roasts. Ryan has opened for the likes of Bill Burr, Yannis Pappas, Cedric the Entertainer, Pablo Francisco, and currently tours with Jack Jr. 


Vic Gjonaj is a comic from Los Angeles and the host of the Early Morning Podcast. Vic can be seen regularly at the Brea Improv and comedy clubs across Southern California. Vic's endless stories about his immigrant upbringing, unfortunate situations and wild events will make you laugh until it hurts.

events.title: Original Tin: Tin Type Photo Shoot
events.description:
Step into history at Original Tin, Brooklyn’s premier wet plate tin type studio, now open at Industry City.
On June 28, we’re celebrating our Industry City debut with a day of 30-minute portrait sessions at a special introductory rate of $125. Walk in or book a time slot in advance.
Tin type is a 19th Century photographic process that produces a one-of-a-kind image on a real metal plate. Each photo is hand poured and developed before your eyes. It’s a magical process with a beautiful, ethereal quality unlike any other. We shoot with vintage large format view cameras and lenses that deliver extraordinary detail and presence you simply can’t get from a digital camera. No files. No filters. Just you — or your pet — rendered in silver on a piece of metal that will outlast us all.
Sessions are limited. Reserve your spot at https://www.originaltin.org/schedule
events.vendor: Original Tin: Tin Type Photo Shoot

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
